title: Chat panel: streaming renderer, thinking blocks, tool call cards
analysis:
The ChatPanel needs to display a streaming Claude conversation with rich message rendering. No chat-related code exists yet — this is a greenfield implementation.

**Architecture:** The core is a `claudeStore` (following the existing `createEngineStore` pattern) that manages message state and session status. It processes `OutboundMessage` events from the sidecar (TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCall, ToolResult, Done, Error) and exposes state for the UI components. The store accepts dependency-injected callbacks for sending messages and aborting, making it fully testable without sidecar IPC.

**Streaming approach:** For 60 FPS rendering, incoming text deltas are buffered in a plain array and flushed to the SolidJS signal via requestAnimationFrame. This prevents re-rendering on every token while maintaining smooth progressive display.

**Markdown:** A lightweight custom markdown renderer (markdown.ts) handles code blocks, inline code, bold, italic, headers, and links. No external dependency needed — the CAD context won't use exotic markdown features. The renderer produces HTML applied via innerHTML on the accumulated (not per-delta) text. HTML entities are escaped before markdown transforms to prevent XSS.

**Component hierarchy:** ChatPanel → MessageGroup[] → { ThinkingBlock?, ToolCallCard[], StreamingText }. The AbortButton replaces the Send button position when sessionStatus is active. Each sub-component lives in `panels/chat/` following the `editor/` subdirectory pattern.

**Sidecar types reuse:** The OutboundMessage types from `gui/sidecar/src/types.ts` define the exact event shapes. The store mirrors these into its own domain types (ToolCallInfo, AssistantMessage) for UI consumption.

**Store state shape:**
- SessionStatus: 'idle' | 'thinking' | 'responding' | 'tool-calling'
- ToolCallInfo: { id, toolName, toolInput, status: 'pending'|'complete'|'error', result? }
- AssistantMessage: { id, thinkingText, thinkingComplete, responseText, toolCalls: ToolCallInfo[], complete, error? }
- ChatMessage: { role: 'user'|'assistant', ... }
- ClaudeState: { messages: ChatMessage[], sessionStatus, currentMessageId }

**Review fix (XSS):** The link renderer in markdown.ts inserts URLs directly into href attributes. Since escapeHtml only neutralizes HTML-special characters, protocol-based attacks (javascript:, data:, vbscript:) pass through unchanged. Fix: add a protocol allowlist (http://, https://, mailto:) before emitting <a> tags; non-matching URLs render as plain text.

**Review fix (tool_result matching):** tool_result events match pending tool calls by toolName only, causing the second of two same-name tool calls to never resolve. Fix: assign locally-unique IDs at push time and match tool_result to the first *pending* entry with the matching toolName, so sequential same-tool calls resolve in FIFO order.

prerequisites.description: Create gui/src/panels/chat/ directory and index.ts barrel export file

Create gui/src/panels/chat/markdown.ts — lightweight markdown-to-HTML renderer supporting code blocks (``` fenced), inline code (`), bold (**), italic (*), headers (#-###), links ([text](url)), and unordered lists (- item). Uses sequential regex transforms on pre-escaped HTML. Returns sanitized HTML string. Include unit tests inline or in a dedicated test.

steps.description: Write failing tests for claudeStore in gui/src/__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts. Test cases: (1) initial state has sessionStatus='idle', empty messages array, currentMessageId=null; (2) handleOutboundMessage with Ready event keeps idle state; (3) sendMessage adds a user ChatMessage to messages, sets currentMessageId, calls onSend callback with message text and context; (4) handleOutboundMessage with TextDelta accumulates responseText on current assistant message, sets sessionStatus='responding'; (5) handleOutboundMessage with ThinkingDelta accumulates thinkingText, sets sessionStatus='thinking'; (6) handleOutboundMessage with ToolCall adds ToolCallInfo entry with status='pending', sets sessionStatus='tool-calling'; (7) handleOutboundMessage with ToolResult updates matching tool call status to 'complete' and stores result; (8) handleOutboundMessage with Done marks assistant message complete, sets sessionStatus='idle'; (9) handleOutboundMessage with ErrorMessage sets error on assistant message, sets sessionStatus='idle'; (10) claudeAbort calls onAbort callback, sets sessionStatus='idle'; (11) clearSession resets messages to empty array.

Implement gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts. createClaudeStore(options: {onSend, onAbort}) factory returning {state, handleOutboundMessage, sendMessage, claudeAbort, clearSession}. State uses createStore<ClaudeState>. Define and export types: SessionStatus, ToolCallInfo, AssistantMessage, UserMessage, ChatMessage, ClaudeState. handleOutboundMessage switches on msg.type and uses produce() for store mutations. sendMessage creates user message, then assistant message shell, calls onSend. Use batch() for grouped state updates.

Write failing tests for claudeStore 60fps delta batching: (1) 'buffers multiple text_delta events and flushes on rAF' — send 10 text_delta events synchronously, verify responseText is empty before rAF flush, mock requestAnimationFrame callback fires, verify responseText contains all 10 deltas concatenated; (2) 'flushes immediately on done event' — send text_delta + done, verify text is flushed without waiting for rAF; (3) 'clears pending buffer on abort'. Use vi.useFakeTimers() and manual rAF mock.

Add requestAnimationFrame-based delta batching to claudeStore: maintain a textBuffer and thinkingBuffer (plain string arrays). On text_delta/thinking_delta, push to buffer and schedule rAF if not already scheduled. rAF callback concatenates buffer contents, applies to store via setState, clears buffers. On done/error, flush buffers immediately (cancel pending rAF). On abort, clear buffers.

Write failing tests for AbortButton in gui/src/__tests__/AbortButton.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='abort-button'; (2) calls onAbort callback on click; (3) has aria-label='Abort generation'; (4) contains a stop icon element (■ character or SVG).

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/AbortButton.tsx and AbortButton.module.css. Props: {onAbort: () => void}. Simple button with ■ stop icon, themed styling matching existing button patterns. Red accent on hover.

Write failing tests for ThinkingBlock in gui/src/__tests__/ThinkingBlock.test.tsx: (1) when complete=false, shows animated indicator with data-testid='thinking-indicator'; (2) when complete=false, does NOT show thinking text content; (3) when complete=true, renders collapsible section with data-testid='thinking-block'; (4) when complete=true, thinking text is NOT visible by default (collapsed); (5) clicking the header expands to show thinking text; (6) clicking again collapses; (7) expanded text container has muted styling (class containing 'muted' or similar).

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/ThinkingBlock.tsx and ThinkingBlock.module.css. Props: {text: string, complete: boolean}. When !complete: three pulsing dots with 'Thinking...' label, CSS keyframe animation. When complete: header row 'Thinking' with ▶/▼ chevron, createSignal(false) for expanded state, Show component wrapping text content. Styling: --reify-text-muted color, 12px font, subtle background.

Write failing tests for ToolCallCard in gui/src/__tests__/ToolCallCard.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='tool-call-card'; (2) displays tool name; (3) shows spinner element when status='pending'; (4) shows checkmark when status='complete'; (5) shows X mark when status='error'; (6) click expands to show full input JSON in data-testid='tool-call-details'; (7) for reify_update_source tool, shows 'View diff' text; (8) for reify_get_parameters with result containing array, shows count summary like '3 parameters'; (9) read tools (reify_get_*) have blue icon class; (10) write tools (reify_update_*) have orange icon class.

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/ToolCallCard.tsx and ToolCallCard.module.css. Props: {toolCall: ToolCallInfo}. Compact card layout: icon (colored circle with first letter or tool-type icon), tool name, param summary (first key-value from toolInput). Expandable details panel with pre-formatted JSON for input and result. Status indicator: CSS spin animation for pending, ✓/✗ characters for complete/error. isReadTool() helper checks toolName.startsWith('reify_get'). Special result summaries for known tools.

Write failing tests for StreamingText in gui/src/__tests__/StreamingText.test.tsx: (1) renders text content with data-testid='streaming-text'; (2) shows blinking cursor with data-testid='streaming-cursor' when streaming=true; (3) hides cursor when streaming=false; (4) applies markdown: bold text (**bold**) renders as <strong>; (5) applies markdown: code block renders as <pre><code>; (6) renders inline code with <code> tags; (7) empty text renders empty container without errors.

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/StreamingText.tsx and StreamingText.module.css. Props: {text: string, streaming: boolean}. createMemo to run renderMarkdown(text) only when text changes. Render via innerHTML on a div. Blinking cursor span with CSS animation (opacity 0↔1, 530ms interval). Code blocks get monospace + subtle background. Prose gets 13px standard font.

Write failing tests for MessageGroup in gui/src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx (grouped with ChatPanel tests since MessageGroup is an internal component): (1) renders ThinkingBlock when message has non-empty thinkingText; (2) does NOT render ThinkingBlock when thinkingText is empty; (3) renders ToolCallCard for each tool call; (4) renders StreamingText for response text; (5) visual order in DOM: thinking-indicator/thinking-block before tool-call-card before streaming-text.

Implement gui/src/panels/chat/MessageGroup.tsx and MessageGroup.module.css. Props: {message: AssistantMessage}. Container div with subtle left-border accent (--reify-accent). Conditionally renders ThinkingBlock (Show when={message.thinkingText}), For loop of ToolCallCard, StreamingText with streaming={!message.complete}. Export from chat/index.ts.

Write failing tests for ChatPanel in gui/src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx: (1) renders with data-testid='chat-panel'; (2) shows empty state message when no messages; (3) renders user message with data-testid='user-message'; (4) renders assistant message via MessageGroup; (5) abort button visible when sessionStatus='responding'; (6) abort button NOT visible when sessionStatus='idle'; (7) send button visible when sessionStatus='idle'; (8) send button disabled when textarea is empty; (9) typing in textarea and clicking send calls onSend; (10) full event sequence: create store, sendMessage, feed text_deltas + done via handleOutboundMessage, verify rendered output.

Implement gui/src/panels/ChatPanel.tsx and ChatPanel.module.css. Layout: flex column, scrollable message list (overflow-y: auto, ref for auto-scroll), fixed input area at bottom. Message list: For loop over store messages, Show conditional for user vs assistant rendering. Input area: textarea with onKeyDown (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline), conditional Send button (idle) vs AbortButton (active). Auto-scroll: createEffect on messages length, scrollTo bottom. Export from panels/index.ts.

Update barrel exports and smoke tests: (1) gui/src/panels/index.ts — add 'export { ChatPanel } from ./ChatPanel'; (2) gui/src/stores/index.ts — add 'export { createClaudeStore } from ./claudeStore' and type exports for ClaudeState, SessionStatus, ToolCallInfo, AssistantMessage; (3) gui/src/__tests__/panels-smoke.test.tsx — add ChatPanel to the smoke test suite with minimal props (store with empty state).

Wire ChatPanel into App.tsx: (1) import createClaudeStore and ChatPanel; (2) create claudeStore instance in App with no-op onSend/onAbort callbacks (actual sidecar IPC wiring comes from a separate integration task); (3) add ChatPanel below ConstraintPanel in the side panel grid, adjusting grid-template-rows to accommodate three rows (property editor + constraint panel + chat panel). Pass claudeStore state and action props.

Write failing tests for markdown.ts link protocol allowlist in gui/src/__tests__/markdown.test.ts. Add a new describe('link protocol validation') block with test cases: (1) 'allows http:// URLs' — renderMarkdown('[link](http://example.com)') contains '<a href="http://example.com"'; (2) 'allows https:// URLs' — renderMarkdown('[link](https://example.com)') contains '<a href="https://example.com"'; (3) 'blocks javascript: URLs' — renderMarkdown('[click](javascript:alert(document.cookie))') does NOT contain 'href="javascript:' and renders the link text as plain text without an <a> tag; (4) 'blocks javascript: with mixed case' — renderMarkdown('[x](JavaScript:alert(1))') does NOT contain '<a'; (5) 'blocks data: URLs' — renderMarkdown('[x](data:text/html,<script>alert(1)</script>)') does NOT contain 'href="data:'; (6) 'blocks vbscript: URLs' — renderMarkdown('[x](vbscript:MsgBox)') does NOT contain '<a'; (7) 'renders blocked link text as plain text' — for a javascript: URL, the link text is still visible in the output but without a hyperlink wrapper; (8) 'allows mailto: URLs' — renderMarkdown('[email](mailto:a@b.com)') contains '<a href="mailto:a@b.com"'.

Fix XSS vulnerability in gui/src/panels/chat/markdown.ts link renderer (lines 108-111). Replace the link regex substitution with a function-based replacer that validates the URL protocol before emitting an tag. Extract a helper function `isSafeUrl(url: string): boolean` that lowercases and checks `url.startsWith('http://') || url.startsWith('https://') || url.startsWith('mailto:')`. In the regex replacer: if isSafeUrl(url) returns true, emit `${text}`; otherwise emit just the plain text (the link text without any hyperlink wrapper). Note: the URL has already been HTML-entity-escaped at this point, so the protocol check must account for that — however http/https/mailto contain no HTML-special characters, so the startsWith check works correctly on escaped input.

Write failing tests for claudeStore tool_result FIFO matching in gui/src/__tests__/claudeStore.test.ts. Add a new describe('tool_result FIFO matching') block with test cases: (1) 'resolves sequential same-tool calls in order' — send two tool_call events with the same tool_name 'reify_get_parameters' (but different tool_input), then send two tool_result events with tool_name 'reify_get_parameters' and different results. Verify the first tool_result updates toolCalls[0] to status='complete' with its result, and the second tool_result updates toolCalls[1] to status='complete' with its result; (2) 'does not re-update an already-completed tool call' — send one tool_call, one tool_result (completes it), then another tool_result with the same tool_name; verify toolCalls[0].result is unchanged (the second result does not overwrite the first); (3) 'tool calls have locally-unique IDs' — send two tool_call events with the same tool_name, verify toolCalls[0].id !== toolCalls[1].id; (4) 'mixed tool names still match correctly' — send tool_call A, tool_call B, tool_call A (same name as first), then results for B, A, A; verify each tool call is matched to its correct result in FIFO order within same-name groups.

Fix tool_result matching logic in gui/src/stores/claudeStore.ts. Two changes: (1) In the tool_call handler (line 149-154), change the ToolCallInfo.id assignment from `msg.id` (the conversation message ID, same for all tool calls in a turn) to a locally-generated unique ID: `${msg.id}-tc-${m.toolCalls.length}`. This ensures each tool call has a distinct id. (2) In the tool_result handler (line 169), change `m.toolCalls.find((t) => t.toolName === msg.tool_name)` to `m.toolCalls.find((t) => t.toolName === msg.tool_name && t.status === 'pending')`. This ensures tool results are matched to the first *pending* entry with the matching tool name, so sequential same-tool calls are resolved in FIFO order rather than all updating the first entry.

design_decisions.decision: Custom lightweight markdown renderer instead of adding a dependency like 'marked'
design_decisions.rationale: The CAD tool context limits markdown usage to code blocks, inline code, bold, italic, headers, and links. A ~100-line regex-based renderer avoids a new dependency, keeps bundle size small, and is fully testable. Can be upgraded later if needed.

design_decisions.decision: requestAnimationFrame-based delta batching in the store, not in the component
design_decisions.rationale: Batching at the store level means all consumers (current and future) automatically get 60fps updates. SolidJS's fine-grained reactivity means the DOM update from a signal change is already efficient — the bottleneck is the frequency of signal writes, which rAF batching solves.

design_decisions.decision: Dependency-injected callbacks (onSend, onAbort) rather than direct sidecar IPC in claudeStore
design_decisions.rationale: Decouples the store from IPC transport, making it fully unit-testable with vi.fn() mocks. The actual IPC wiring happens in App.tsx (or a future bridge integration), following the same pattern as engineStore's subscribeToEvents.

Five sub-components (StreamingText, ThinkingBlock, ToolCallCard, AbortButton, MessageGroup) plus their CSS modules would clutter the flat panels/ directory. The editor/ subdirectory pattern establishes precedent for this approach.

design_decisions.decision: Store processes OutboundMessage directly rather than re-mapping types
design_decisions.rationale: The sidecar OutboundMessage types (TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCall, ToolResult, Done, ErrorMessage) are imported from gui/sidecar/src/types.ts. Using them directly avoids a redundant mapping layer. The store's internal AssistantMessage/ToolCallInfo types are the domain model for UI consumption.

Thinking text is verbose internal reasoning not usually relevant to the user. Showing it collapsed keeps the conversation readable while preserving access for debugging. During streaming, an animated indicator signals activity without taking space.

design_decisions.decision: Color-coded tool icons: blue for read operations, orange for write operations
design_decisions.rationale: Provides instant visual distinction between safe (read) and mutating (write) tool calls. Aligns with common UI conventions (blue=info, orange=warning/action). The reify_get_* vs reify_update_* naming makes classification straightforward.

design_decisions.decision: innerHTML for markdown rendering with pre-escaped HTML entities
design_decisions.rationale: SolidJS uses innerHTML for injecting HTML content. Since our custom renderer escapes all HTML entities (< > & " ') BEFORE applying markdown regex transforms, and Claude output doesn't contain user-supplied HTML, XSS risk is negligible. The escape-first approach is simpler than a full sanitizer.

design_decisions.decision: ChatPanel receives store as a prop rather than using a global context/provider
design_decisions.rationale: Following the existing pattern where App.tsx creates store instances and passes them as props (see engineStore, editorStore, selectionStore usage). This keeps stores explicit, testable, and avoids hidden dependencies via context.

design_decisions.decision: Protocol allowlist for markdown link URLs instead of blocklist
design_decisions.rationale: An allowlist (http, https, mailto) is safer than a blocklist (javascript, data, vbscript, ...) because it protects against unknown future attack vectors. Any URL that doesn't match the allowlist is rendered as plain text — the link text remains visible, only the hyperlink wrapper is removed. This is a defense-in-depth measure since Claude output is attacker-controllable via prompt injection through CAD file content.

design_decisions.decision: FIFO matching for tool_result events using pending-status filter instead of unique wire IDs
design_decisions.rationale: The sidecar wire protocol's ToolResult.id field carries the conversation-message ID (same for all tool calls in a turn), providing no per-call disambiguator. Rather than requiring a wire protocol change, the store assigns locally-unique IDs at push time (${msg.id}-tc-${index}) and matches tool_results to the first *pending* entry with the matching toolName. This resolves sequential same-tool calls in FIFO order without any protocol changes.

reuse.what: createStore + produce + batch pattern from solid-js/store
reuse.how: claudeStore follows the exact same factory pattern: createClaudeStore() returns {state, ...actions}. Uses createStore for reactive state, produce for immutable-style mutations, batch for grouped updates.

reuse.what: OutboundMessage types (TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCall, ToolResult, Done, ErrorMessage, Ready)
reuse.how: Import directly into claudeStore.ts as the event types the store processes. No need to duplicate these type definitions.
All new CSS modules use the same CSS custom properties for consistent theming. No new color values — reuse existing palette.

reuse.what: Expand/collapse toggle pattern with createSignal + Show
reuse.how: ThinkingBlock and ToolCallCard use the same toggle pattern: createSignal(boolean) for expanded state, ▶/▼ chevron indicator, Show component for conditional content rendering.

reuse.what: data-testid convention and vitest + @solidjs/testing-library test patterns
reuse.how: All new components use data-testid attributes. Tests use render(() => <Component />), screen.getByTestId(), fireEvent.click(), vi.fn(), vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.advanceTimersByTime() patterns.

reuse.what: Component<Props> with CSS module imports pattern
reuse.how: All new components follow: import styles from './X.module.css', export const X: Component<XProps> = (props) => { ... }.
Add ChatPanel to panels/index.ts and createClaudeStore to stores/index.ts following existing named export patterns.

reuse.what: Status indicator pattern with data-status attribute for CSS styling
reuse.how: ToolCallCard status indicator uses the same data-status attribute pattern: [data-status='pending'], [data-status='complete'], [data-status='error'] for CSS color styling.
ToolCallCard pending status reuses the same spin animation pattern (border with transparent top, rotate 360deg).
